---
output:
  md_document:
    variant: gfm
---

```{r setup, echo = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "README-",
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 5,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)

library(sizeMat)
```

# sizeMat

<!-- badges: start -->
[![packageversion](https://img.shields.io/badge/Package%20version-1.2.0-orange.svg?style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ejosymart/sizeMat)
[![CRAN status](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/sizeMat)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=sizeMat)
[![CRAN downloads](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/sizeMat)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=sizeMat)
[![Total downloads](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/grand-total/sizeMat)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=sizeMat)
[![R version](https://img.shields.io/badge/R%3E%3D-4.0.0-6666ff.svg)](https://cran.r-project.org/)
<!-- badges: end -->

<img src="man/figures/sizeMat.png" align="right" height="140" />

**Estimate Size at Sexual Maturity**

`sizeMat` is an R package for estimating size at morphometric and gonadal maturity in organisms, especially fish and invertebrates.

The package includes tools for:

- classifying individuals into juvenile and adult groups using relative growth;
- estimating size at morphometric maturity;
- estimating size at gonadal maturity;
- fitting maturity ogives using frequentist or Bayesian logistic regression;
- visualizing results using base R graphics or optional `ggplot2`-style plots.

The size at sexual maturity is commonly expressed as $L_{50}$, the size at which an individual has a 50% probability of being mature.

## Installation

Install the released version from CRAN:

```{r install-cran, eval = FALSE}
install.packages("sizeMat")
```

Install the development version from GitHub:

```{r install-github, eval = FALSE}
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("ejosymart/sizeMat")
```

## Example 1: Size at morphometric maturity

The morphometric maturity workflow has two main steps:

1. classify individuals as juveniles or adults using two allometric variables;
2. estimate the maturity ogive and $L_{50}$.

```{r morph-classify}
data(crabdata)

classify_data <- classify_mature(
  crabdata,
  varNames = c("carapace_width", "chela_height"),
  varSex = "sex_category",
  selectSex = NULL,
  method = "ld"
)

print(classify_data)
```

### Base R plot

By default, `plot.classify()` uses base R graphics.

```{r morph-classify-base}
plot(
  classify_data,
  xlab = "Carapace width (mm)",
  ylab = "Chela height (mm)"
)
```

### ggplot2-style plot

Use `gg_style = TRUE` to return a `ggplot` object.

```{r morph-classify-gg}
plot(
  classify_data,
  xlab = "Carapace width (mm)",
  ylab = "Chela height (mm)",
  col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),
  pch = c(16, 17),
  gg_style = TRUE
)
```

Because the output is a `ggplot` object, it can be modified using `ggplot2`.

```{r morph-classify-gg-custom}
p_classify <- plot(
  classify_data,
  xlab = "Carapace width (mm)",
  ylab = "Chela height (mm)",
  col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),
  pch = c(16, 17),
  gg_style = TRUE
)

p_classify +
  ggplot2::theme_bw()
```

### Estimate morphometric maturity

```{r morph-estimate}
set.seed(123)

my_morph <- morph_mature(
  classify_data,
  method = "fq",
  niter = 200
)

print(my_morph)
```

### Base R maturity ogive

```{r morph-ogive-base}
plot(
  my_morph,
  xlab = "Carapace width (mm)",
  ylab = "Proportion mature",
  col = c("blue", "red"),
  onlyOgive = TRUE
)
```

### ggplot2-style maturity ogive

```{r morph-ogive-gg}
plot(
  my_morph,
  xlab = "Carapace width (mm)",
  ylab = "Proportion mature",
  col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),
  onlyOgive = TRUE,
  gg_style = TRUE
)
```

When `gg_style = TRUE` and `onlyOgive = FALSE`, the plot method returns a list of independent `ggplot` objects.

```{r morph-gg-list}
p_morph <- plot(
  my_morph,
  xlab = "Carapace width (mm)",
  ylab = "Proportion mature",
  col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),
  gg_style = TRUE
)

names(p_morph)
```

Each plot can be displayed separately.

```{r morph-gg-l50}
p_morph$L50
```

```{r morph-gg-ogive-custom}
p_morph$ogive +
  ggplot2::theme_bw() +
  ggplot2::ggtitle("Morphometric maturity ogive")
```

## Example 2: Size at gonadal maturity

The gonadal maturity workflow uses one allometric variable and a maturity stage variable.

```{r gonad-estimate}
data(matFish)

set.seed(123)

my_gonad <- gonad_mature(
  matFish,
  varNames = c("total_length", "stage_mat"),
  immName = "I",
  matName = c("II", "III", "IV"),
  method = "fq",
  niter = 200
)

print(my_gonad)
```

### Base R maturity ogive

```{r gonad-ogive-base}
plot(
  my_gonad,
  xlab = "Total length (cm)",
  ylab = "Proportion mature",
  col = c("blue", "red"),
  onlyOgive = TRUE
)
```

The legend position can be modified.

```{r gonad-ogive-base-legend}
plot(
  my_gonad,
  xlab = "Total length (cm)",
  ylab = "Proportion mature",
  col = c("blue", "red"),
  onlyOgive = TRUE,
  legendPosition = "bottomright"
)
```

### ggplot2-style maturity ogive

```{r gonad-ogive-gg}
plot(
  my_gonad,
  xlab = "Total length (cm)",
  ylab = "Proportion mature",
  col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),
  onlyOgive = TRUE,
  gg_style = TRUE
)
```

When `gg_style = TRUE` and `onlyOgive = FALSE`, the function returns a list of independent `ggplot` objects.

```{r gonad-gg-list}
p_gonad <- plot(
  my_gonad,
  xlab = "Total length (cm)",
  ylab = "Proportion mature",
  col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),
  gg_style = TRUE
)

names(p_gonad)
```

```{r gonad-gg-l50}
p_gonad$L50
```

```{r gonad-gg-ogive-custom}
p_gonad$ogive +
  ggplot2::theme_bw() +
  ggplot2::ggtitle("Gonadal maturity ogive")
```

## Combining ggplot2-style outputs

The elements returned by `plot(..., gg_style = TRUE)` are standard `ggplot` objects. They can be combined using external packages such as `ggpubr` or `cowplot`.

```{r combine-ggpubr, eval = requireNamespace("ggpubr", quietly = TRUE), fig.width = 9, fig.height = 7}
ggpubr::ggarrange(
  p_gonad$A,
  p_gonad$B,
  p_gonad$L50,
  p_gonad$ogive,
  ncol = 2,
  nrow = 2
)
```

```{r combine-cowplot, eval = requireNamespace("cowplot", quietly = TRUE), fig.width = 9, fig.height = 7}
cowplot::plot_grid(
  p_gonad$A,
  p_gonad$B,
  p_gonad$L50,
  p_gonad$ogive,
  ncol = 2
)
```

## Citation

To cite `sizeMat`, use:

```{r citation}
citation("sizeMat")
```
